title: A Meteor Just Exploded Over Rome — and Cameras Caught It All
script: A meteor exploded in the sky over Rome and dozens of doorbell cams caught the flash. The fireball blew up 50 miles above the city around 9 PM local time. Astronomers say it was probably the size of a basketball. The sonic boom rattled windows across three regions. Nobody got hurt, and scientists are now combing fields outside Rome hoping to find a fragment. Earth gets hit by 17 of these every day.
